summary: Fighter cleanup pass focused on match-preserving symbol/prototype clarification across fighter and HSD baselib code. The PR renamed several address-style symbols to semantic names, propagated those names through assembly callsites and headers, replaced hard-coded fighter/character table length 33 with FTKIND_MAX/CHKIND_MAX, exposed HSD/baselib prototypes needed by fighter code, and clarified a fighter data table comment. The author noted these commits were manually cherry-picked from roughly PR #636 and checked for matching, so the value was mainly improving decomp readability and future C migration without changing generated code intent.
what_changed: Renamed the assembly symbol func_8006DE00 to Fighter_GetNextJointInTree and updated many fighter assembly callsites in ftanim.s, ftparts.s, ftkirby.s, and ft_unknown_006.s.
Renamed lbl_803C6980 to ftMObj and propagated the name through assembly references that load the object/class table address.

Renamed HSD/baselib assembly symbols func_8036E04C to HSD_PObjSetMtxMark, func_8036E080 to HSD_PObjGetMtxMark, func_80379A20 to HSD_MtxInverseTranspose, and func_803742AC to _HSD_mkEnvelopeModelNodeMtx, with matching header declarations.

Added or exposed baselib prototypes and externs including hsdChangeClass, hsdObjIsDescendantOf, HSD_JObjSetDefaultClass, HSD_JObjRemove, HSD_JObjGetDObj, HSD_JObjSetCurrent, HSD_JObjGetCurrent, HSD_MObjGetTObj, HSD_MObjRemove, hsdPObj, HSD_PObjSetDefaultClass, HSD_PObjClearMtxMark, HSD_PObjSetMtxMark, and HSD_PObjGetMtxMark.
In fighter.c, replaced hard-coded 33-element fighter arrays and loops with FTKIND_MAX for callbacks, costume/data/action-state tables, and iteration.
In fighter.h, added FTKIND_MAX = FTKIND_NONE and CHKIND_MAX = CHKIND_NONE enum aliases, and exported several HSD_ObjAllocData globals including fighter_bones_alloc_data.
Renamed lbl_80459028 to fighter_bones_alloc_data and updated its initialization/free usage plus assembly references.
In ftdata.h, changed lbl_803C20CC from a hard-coded 66-entry table to FTKIND_MAX and added lbl_803C2150 as an FTKIND_MAX-sized visibility callback table.
Changed the ftdata.s comment for lbl_803C2150 from an incorrect or stale multi-jump-table description to character model group visibility change callbacks.
Updated lbRefract_PObjLoad prototype from an unnamed single HSD_PObj* parameter to s32 lbRefract_PObjLoad(HSD_PObj* pobj, HSD_PObjDesc* desc).
Made minor formatting/comment cleanup in item.h.
smart_moves: Performed semantic renames directly in assembly while preserving instruction bytes and branch targets, making progress useful before full C decompilation of those files.
Propagated renamed symbols consistently through callsites and headers, reducing the chance that future C translations continue using placeholder func_/lbl_ names.
Introduced FTKIND_MAX and CHKIND_MAX enum aliases instead of leaving magic array bound 33, making fighter table sizing self-documenting and safer for later table audits.
Kept the PR scoped as a cleanup/matching pass: the body says commits were manually cherry-picked and each was checked to match, which is appropriate for symbol/header cleanup in a decomp project.
Added baselib declarations where fighter code already depended on HSD behavior, helping future files include typed APIs instead of local extern guesses.
Corrected an apparently misleading data-table comment, which is valuable because table purpose drives later naming and struct recovery.
decomp_lessons: When a numeric constant is really an enum-table bound, add a MAX alias tied to the sentinel rather than scattering the literal through C declarations and loops.
Assembly-only files can still benefit from semantic symbol names; renaming labels and call targets improves cross-file searchability without requiring a full C match first.
For HSD/baselib functions, prefer library-style names such as HSD_PObjGetMtxMark and HSD_MtxInverseTranspose once behavior is identified, then update headers and callsites together.
Global allocator data should be named after the allocated domain, e.g. fighter_bones_alloc_data, because allocator globals often appear both in C and assembly references.
Data table comments should be treated as decomp evidence: an incorrect table-purpose comment can mislead future struct/function naming, so fixing it is part of cleanup.
When cherry-picking decomp cleanup from another branch or PR, explicitly verify matching after each commit or after the manual port, because history divergence can hide accidental codegen changes.
naming_conventions: Fighter-specific functions use the Fighter_ prefix, as in Fighter_GetNextJointInTree.
HSD baselib APIs use HSD_<Class><Action> style, as in HSD_PObjGetMtxMark, HSD_PObjSetMtxMark, HSD_MtxInverseTranspose, HSD_JObjGetCurrent, and HSD_MObjRemove.
Internal HSD helper names may keep a leading underscore, as in _HSD_mkEnvelopeModelNodeMtx.
Fighter enum bounds use MAX aliases assigned to the NONE sentinel: FTKIND_MAX = FTKIND_NONE and CHKIND_MAX = CHKIND_NONE.
Descriptive global data names are preferred over lbl_ addresses when purpose is known, e.g. lbl_80459028 became fighter_bones_alloc_data and lbl_803C6980 became ftMObj.
Callback/function pointer table declarations should use the enum-derived bound FTKIND_MAX instead of literal 33/66 where supported by the recovered model.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Renamed assembly labels and branch-link targets while leaving addresses/opcodes unchanged, e.g. func_8006DE00 -> Fighter_GetNextJointInTree at 0x8006DE00.
Propagated global label renames in lis/addi address loads, e.g. lbl_803C6980@ha/@l -> ftMObj@ha/@l.
Matched HSD library function names across asm and C declarations: func_8036E080 -> HSD_PObjGetMtxMark, func_8036E04C -> HSD_PObjSetMtxMark, func_80379A20 -> HSD_MtxInverseTranspose.
Used header prototypes to lock in inferred signatures, such as Fighter_GetNextJointInTree(HSD_Joint** pjoint, s32* pdepth) and HSD_PObjGetMtxMark(int idx, void** obj, u32* mark).
Replaced hard-coded table sizes with enum constants in C while keeping intent matchable and readable.
The PR body explicitly says commits were manually cherry-picked and checked for matching, signaling a match-preservation workflow rather than speculative refactoring.
review_feedback: No inline review comments were present in the provided PR slice.
The only recorded review was an approval by r-burns on 2023-01-06 with an empty body.
searchable_terms: character model group visibility change callbacks
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that decompile Fighter_GetNextJointInTree or replace its assembly implementation with C.
Search for remaining hard-coded fighter table bounds 33 or 66 after FTKIND_MAX/CHKIND_MAX were introduced.
Audit remaining references to lbl_803C2150 to confirm the character model group visibility callback interpretation.
Search for remaining func_8036E04C, func_8036E080, func_80379A20, and func_803742AC aliases to ensure all callsites use the HSD names.
Inspect later baselib PRs for finalized signatures of HSD_PObjGetMtxMark, HSD_PObjSetMtxMark, HSD_MtxInverseTranspose, and _HSD_mkEnvelopeModelNodeMtx.
Look for subsequent cleanup of fighter_bones_alloc_data companion allocator globals still named lbl_80458FFC/lbl_80459054/lbl_80459080/lbl_804590AC.
